【Day 2】Hooked on the First Login — Walking into the Web through Slime's First Home
Code Review of My Youth - From the BIOS Beep to Software Engineering

Early on, the computer was just "a box that opens up to play games" to me. Until one day I ran into a really grumpy older cousin who showed me something on the screen that wasn't a game — a website called "Slime's First Home". That's where my internet world began.
【Day 1】The First 'Beep' — Where My Computer World Begins

Do you remember the first time you touched a computer? For me, it was a memory mixed with mystery, taboo, and pure excitement. From the first "beep" of a boot sequence to learning to open MS-DOS, run games, and tweak config.sys — I went from a kid hooked on computers to walking the road to becoming a software engineer.
What Is a Port? Why Does Everyone Use 3000? A Practical Guide to Picking Ports

A port is how your machine tells network services apart — and 3000 is just a safe, unreserved default that dev tools standardised on. This post covers what ports are, how the reserved ranges work, how to pick one for your project, and how to find out what's hogging a port.
Build Your Own MCP Server for AI Agents — A Complete Implementation Guide With GitHub Copilot Chat

MCP Server is incredibly popular right now, and major service providers are releasing their own MCP Servers. So to better understand how MCP Server works, what better way than to build one yourself? This post takes you from zero to a working MCP Server, paired with GitHub Copilot Chat.
GitHub Copilot + MCP Server in VSCode: Build Your AI Dev Workflow

AI development has become a must-have skill for engineers, but when we use AI tools we often hit the same issues: the AI returns something we didn't expect, or it just doesn't get what we're asking for. These problems seriously hurt productivity. To solve this, MCP Server came along.
Google Domains Shut Down — Buying a Domain on Cloudflare and Deploying a Site, Step by Step

I wrote a domain-buying tutorial way back, using Google Domain as the example. But Google Domain announced shutdown on September 7, 2023, so here's the Cloudflare Domain version — registering a domain, deploying a static site with Pages, and migrating an existing Squarespace (post-Google-Domain) domain over.
No Postman? No Problem — Testing APIs with curl
Sometimes you can't use Postman to test an API and you can't install other tools — maybe you're SSH'd into a server (servers usually have no GUI). That's when you reach for curl.
Build Your Own AI Language Model Lab — No Help Needed, with LM Studio

Most of us have been bombarded by AI, ChatGPT, and other large language models for a while now, and we've all been using web services like ChatGPT or Claude.AI. The upside of these services is that they're easy to start with and convenient. The downsides? You need an internet connection, there are data privacy concerns, and there are API costs. So is there a way to self-host AI locally? Yep — and this post introduces a really handy tool for that called LM Studio.
Heard of LLM, RAG, Prompt? A Quick Guide to Common Generative AI Terms

Generative AI isn't just engineer territory anymore — it's seeping into our daily lives. If you use ChatGPT, Grok, Midjourney, or similar tools, these technical terms will start showing up in your world. If you're an engineer, I'd highly recommend understanding them — future development will lean even more on these technologies to build better products.
The Browser Render Pipeline Every Frontend Engineer Should Know

A student asked me about this recently, and I think it's actually important stuff for frontend engineers. We're constantly working with browsers — not knowing how they work seems like a missed opportunity.
